name: Voter Registration
simple_explanation: To vote in India, your name must be on the Electoral Roll. You can register online at voters.eci.gov.in using Form 6.
simple_hi: भारत में वोट देने के लिए, आपका नाम मतदाता सूची में होना चाहिए। आप फॉर्म 6 का उपयोग करके voters.eci.gov.in पर ऑनलाइन पंजीकरण कर सकते हैं।
simple_mr: भारतात मतदान करण्यासाठी तुमचे नाव मतदार यादीत असणे आवश्यक आहे. तुम्ही फॉर्म ६ वापरून voters.eci.gov.in वर ऑनलाइन नोंदणी करू शकता.
detailed_explanation: The Electoral Roll is a comprehensive database maintained by the Election Commission of India listing all eligible voters in each polling area. New voters fill Form 6 on the National Voters' Service Portal (NVSP). Once approved, you receive your Elector's Photo Identity Card (EPIC). The roll is updated regularly, with major revisions typically around January 1st each year.
why_it_matters: Without your name on the Electoral Roll, you legally cannot vote — even if you are a citizen. Registering is the first and most essential step in exercising your democratic right.
common_doubt_q: I turned 18 last month. Can I vote in the upcoming election?
common_doubt_a: Yes, if you register before the cutoff. The qualifying dates are January 1st, April 1st, July 1st, and October 1st. Apply well in advance as verification takes 2-4 weeks.
key_facts: Form 6 is for new voter registration
EPIC card = Elector's Photo Identity Card (Voter ID)
voters.eci.gov.in is the official NVSP portal
BLO = Booth Level Officer who verifies your details
Electoral Roll is updated with January 1 as qualifying date

mini_quiz.question: What form must a new voter fill to register?
mini_quiz.explanation: Form 6 is the application form for inclusion in the Electoral Roll for new voters. Form 7 is for deletion, Form 8 for corrections.

name: Electoral Roll & EPIC Card
name_hi: मतदाता सूची और मतदाता पहचान पत्र
simple_explanation: The Electoral Roll is the list of all voters. The EPIC card is your voter ID.
simple_hi: मतदाता सूची सभी मतदाताओं की सूची है। EPIC कार्ड आपका मतदाता पहचान पत्र है।
simple_mr: मतदार यादी ही सर्व मतदारांची यादी आहे. EPIC कार्ड हे तुमचे मतदार ओळखपत्र आहे.
detailed_explanation: The Electoral Roll (also called Voter List) is organized by polling booth (typically 1,500 voters per booth). The EPIC card carries your photo, name, EPIC number, and constituency details. Even with your EPIC card, if your name is not on the roll for your current address, you may be turned away.
why_it_matters: The Electoral Roll determines who can vote in each booth. Always verify your name on the roll before election day at electoralsearch.eci.gov.in.
common_doubt_q: Can I vote if I have a Voter ID but my name is not in the list?
common_doubt_a: No. Your name MUST be in the Electoral Roll of that specific polling station to cast a vote.
key_facts: EPIC is a valid ID proof for many services
electoralsearch.eci.gov.in is where you check your name

mini_quiz.question: How many voters are typically assigned to one polling booth?
mini_quiz.explanation: Each polling booth is typically assigned approximately 1,500 voters to manage polling day efficiently.

name: Election Announcement
simple_explanation: ECI announces the schedule, which triggers the Model Code of Conduct.
simple_hi: ECI चुनाव कार्यक्रम की घोषणा करता है, जो आदर्श आचार संहिता को सक्रिय करता है।
simple_mr: निवडणूक आयोग वेळापत्रक जाहीर करतो, ज्यामुळे आचारसंहिता लागू होते.
detailed_explanation: General Elections are announced by the ECI through a Press Note. This triggers the Model Code of Conduct (MCC) immediately. The announcement includes dates for nominations, scrutiny, withdrawal, polling, and counting.
why_it_matters: The announcement marks the start of the official election process and restricts government actions that could influence voters.
common_doubt_q: When does the Model Code of Conduct start?
common_doubt_a: It starts immediately from the moment ECI announces the election schedule.
key_facts: Schedule includes notification and polling dates
Announcement is at least 3 weeks before first polling

mini_quiz.question: What is triggered immediately when the election schedule is announced?
options: Model Code of Conduct comes into force
mini_quiz.explanation: The Model Code of Conduct (MCC) comes into force the moment the election schedule is announced by the ECI.

name: Nomination Filing
simple_explanation: Candidates file their papers and pay a security deposit.
simple_hi: उम्मीदवार अपने कागजात दाखिल करते हैं और सुरक्षा राशि जमा करते हैं।
simple_mr: उमेदवार त्यांची कागदपत्रे दाखल करतात आणि अनामत रक्कम भरतात.
detailed_explanation: Any citizen (25+ for Lok Sabha) can file nomination with the Returning Officer. They must submit an affidavit (Form 26) disclosing criminal records, assets, and education. A security deposit of ₹25,000 (₹12,500 for SC/ST) is required.
why_it_matters: The affidavit allows voters to make an informed choice by knowing the candidate's background.
common_doubt_q: What is the minimum age to contest Lok Sabha?
common_doubt_a: The minimum age is 25 years for Lok Sabha and Vidhan Sabha.
key_facts: Security deposit is ₹25,000 for Lok Sabha

mini_quiz.question: What is the minimum age to contest a Lok Sabha election?
mini_quiz.explanation: Article 84(b) states that a candidate for Lok Sabha must be at least 25 years old.

name: Scrutiny of Nominations
simple_explanation: The Returning Officer checks if nomination papers are valid.
simple_hi: रिटर्निंग ऑफिसर जांचता है कि नामांकन पत्र वैध हैं या नहीं।
simple_mr: निवडणूक निर्णय अधिकारी नामांकन पत्रे वैध आहेत की नाही हे तपासतात.
detailed_explanation: The RO examines every nomination to ensure the candidate is qualified and the forms are complete. Disqualifications include prison sentences of 2+ years, holding office of profit, or being of unsound mind.
why_it_matters: Scrutiny ensures that only legally eligible candidates enter the election fray.
common_doubt_a: The Returning Officer (RO), who is typically a senior government official.
key_facts: Candidates can object to others' papers
Scrutiny happens on a fixed date
Rejected candidates can appeal to High Court

mini_quiz.question: Who conducts the scrutiny of nomination papers?
mini_quiz.explanation: The Returning Officer (RO) is responsible for conducting scrutiny of nominations.

name: Withdrawal of Candidacy
simple_explanation: Candidates can choose to withdraw their name from the election.
simple_hi: उम्मीदवार चुनाव से अपना नाम वापस लेने का विकल्प चुन सकते हैं।
simple_mr: उमेदवार निवडणुकीतून आपले नाव मागे घेण्याचा पर्याय निवडू शकतात.
detailed_explanation: There is a 2-day window for candidates to withdraw. Once withdrawn, it is irrevocable. After this, symbols are allotted and the final list of candidates is published.
why_it_matters: The final list determines the names and symbols that will appear on the EVM.
common_doubt_q: Can a candidate re-enter after withdrawing?
common_doubt_a: No. Once a candidate withdraws, it is final and irrevocable for that election.

mini_quiz.question: Can a candidate withdraw their candidacy after the withdrawal deadline?
mini_quiz.explanation: Once the withdrawal deadline passes, no candidate can withdraw. The final candidate list is published.

name: Election Campaigning
simple_explanation: Candidates reach out to voters through rallies and advertisements.
simple_hi: उम्मीदवार रैलियों और विज्ञापनों के माध्यम से मतदाताओं तक पहुँचते हैं।
simple_mr: उमेदवार रॅली आणि जाहिरातींच्या माध्यमातून मतदारांपर्यंत पोहोचतात.
detailed_explanation: Campaigning includes rallies, door-to-door visits, and social media. MCC rules apply: no use of government resources, no hate speech, and strict spending limits (₹95 lakh for Lok Sabha).
why_it_matters: Campaigning is how voters learn about different candidates' visions and manifestos.
common_doubt_q: Is there a limit on how much a candidate can spend?
common_doubt_a: Yes. As of 2022, the limit is ₹95 lakh for Lok Sabha and ₹40 lakh for Vidhan Sabha candidates.
key_facts: No campaigning 48 hours before poll ends
Paid news is monitored by ECI

mini_quiz.question: What is the maximum campaign expenditure limit for a Lok Sabha candidate (2022)?
mini_quiz.explanation: ECI revised the expenditure limit to ₹95 lakh for Lok Sabha candidates in January 2022.

name: Model Code of Conduct
simple_explanation: Rules that parties must follow to ensure fair elections.
simple_hi: नियम जिनका दलों को निष्पक्ष चुनाव सुनिश्चित करने के लिए पालन करना चाहिए।
simple_mr: निवडणुका निष्पक्ष व्हाव्यात यासाठी पक्षांनी पाळायचे नियम.
detailed_explanation: MCC prevents ruling parties from using government machinery for campaigning. No new schemes can be announced. It ensures a 'level playing field' for all candidates.
why_it_matters: MCC prevents abuse of power and ensures that elections are free and fair.
common_doubt_q: Is MCC a law passed by Parliament?
common_doubt_a: No, it is a consensus-based set of guidelines issued by ECI, but enforced strictly through executive action.
key_facts: Prevents government from announcing new projects

mini_quiz.question: When does the Model Code of Conduct come into force?
options: From the day of election announcement
mini_quiz.explanation: The MCC comes into force immediately from the date ECI announces the election schedule.

name: Silent Period
simple_explanation: A 48-hour blackout on campaigning before polling ends.
simple_hi: मतदान समाप्त होने से 48 घंटे पहले प्रचार पर रोक।
detailed_explanation: Under Section 126 of RPA 1951, active campaigning stops 48 hours before polling ends. This gives voters a 'silent period' to think calmly before voting.
why_it_matters: It prevents last-minute influence or communal tension right before voting.
common_doubt_q: Does this apply to social media?
common_doubt_a: Yes, electronic media is restricted, and ECI has guidelines for social media platforms too.

mini_quiz.question: Under which law is the 48-hour campaign blackout mandated?
mini_quiz.explanation: Section 126 of the Representation of the People Act, 1951 prohibits public meetings during the 48 hours preceding the close of polling.

name: Polling Day
simple_explanation: Voters go to the booth, get marked with ink, and cast their vote.
simple_hi: मतदाता बूथ पर जाते हैं, स्याही लगवाते हैं और अपना वोट डालते हैं।
simple_mr: मतदार बूथवर जातात, शाई लावून घेतात आणि मतदान करतात.
detailed_explanation: Voters carry ID, get verified, receive indelible ink on the left index finger, and press the button on the EVM. VVPAT shows the slip for 7 seconds to confirm the vote.
why_it_matters: This is the core of democracy — where the citizen exercises their power to choose their representative.
common_doubt_q: What if I don't have my Voter ID card?
common_doubt_a: You can use any of the 12 alternative documents like Aadhaar, Passport, or PAN card if your name is in the roll.
key_facts: Indelible ink on left index finger

mini_quiz.question: For how many seconds is the VVPAT slip visible to the voter?
mini_quiz.explanation: The VVPAT slip is visible to the voter for 7 seconds through the transparent window.

name: Counting Day
simple_explanation: Votes from all EVMs are counted and results are declared.
simple_hi: सभी EVM के वोटों की गिनती की जाती है और परिणाम घोषित किए जाते हैं।
simple_mr: सर्व ईव्हीएममधील मतांची मोजणी केली जाते आणि निकाल जाहीर केले जातात.
detailed_explanation: Counting is done under RO supervision. Postal ballots are counted first, then EVM votes round by round. India uses the First-Past-The-Post (FPTP) system.
why_it_matters: Counting translates the will of the voters into a final result.
common_doubt_a: It means the candidate with the most votes wins, even if they don't have more than 50% of the total votes.

mini_quiz.question: What voting system does India use to determine the winner in Lok Sabha?
mini_quiz.explanation: India uses FPTP — the candidate with the most votes in a constituency wins.

name: Results & Government Formation
simple_explanation: The party with 272+ seats forms the central government.
simple_hi: 272+ सीटों वाला दल केंद्र सरकार बनाता है।
simple_mr: २७२+ जागा मिळवणारा पक्ष केंद्र सरकार स्थापन करतो.
detailed_explanation: For Lok Sabha, a party/alliance needs 272/543 seats for a majority. The President invites the majority leader to become PM. If no majority, a coalition or hung parliament occurs.
why_it_matters: This completes the democratic cycle, resulting in a representative government for the next 5 years.
common_doubt_q: How many seats are needed for majority in Lok Sabha?
common_doubt_a: A simple majority requires at least 272 out of 543 elected seats.
key_facts: Term of Lok Sabha is 5 years

mini_quiz.question: How many seats does a party need to win an outright majority in the 543-seat Lok Sabha?
mini_quiz.explanation: A simple majority in the Lok Sabha requires winning more than half of 543 seats = at least 272 seats.
